title: Azure Logic Apps - ARM Template Structure, $connections Deployment, and Definition Parameters
applies_to: Deploying Logic Apps via ARM templates (infrastructure as code)
Configuring managed connector $connections in ARM deployments
Using workflow definition parameters for configurable values

confirmed_facts.fact: The ARM resource type for a Logic App is Microsoft.Logic/workflows (apiVersion: 2019-05-01). The workflow definition (the JSON you edit in code view) lives in properties.definition. Connection values live in properties.parameters.$connections.value.

Managed connector connections are separate ARM resources of type Microsoft.Web/connections (apiVersion: 2016-06-01). The Logic App resource must dependsOn each connection resource so the connection is created before the workflow is deployed.

The $connections.value entry for each connector requires exactly three fields: connectionId (ARM resource ID of the Microsoft.Web/connections instance), connectionName (short name string), and id (managed API catalog ARM ID). Missing any field causes a runtime authentication failure.

The managed API catalog id uses subscriptionResourceId('Microsoft.Web/locations/managedApis', location, connectorName) in ARM templates. This resolves to /subscriptions/{sub}/providers/Microsoft.Web/locations/{region}/managedApis/{connectorName}.
The connectionId uses resourceId('Microsoft.Web/connections', connectionName) in ARM templates. This resolves to /subscriptions/{sub}/resourceGroups/{rg}/providers/Microsoft.Web/connections/{name}.

OAuth-based connections (SharePoint, Office 365, Teams) cannot be fully automated in ARM — a human must authorize the connection after deployment. MSI-based connections (Sentinel, WDATP, Blob with MSI) can be fully automated.

Workflow definition parameters live in definition.parameters with type and optional defaultValue. They are different from ARM template parameters. Reference them in actions as @parameters('ParamName'). Supported types: string, securestring, int, float, bool, array, object, secureobject.
securestring and secureobject definition parameter values are automatically redacted from run history. Use these types for any credential or sensitive config value passed into the workflow.

To enable a System-assigned managed identity on a Logic App ARM resource, set identity.type: 'SystemAssigned'. The principalId and tenantId are populated after deployment and available via reference().
allowedValues on a definition parameter restricts accepted values in the designer for authoring validation only. It is NOT enforced at runtime. Do not rely on it for security.

arm_template_full_example.description: Complete ARM template deploying a Logic App with a Blob Storage connection and MSI

connections_block_structure.description: The $connections value block: connectionId, connectionName, and id are all required.

field_descriptions.connectionId: ARM resource ID of the Microsoft.Web/connections instance — the specific connection
field_descriptions.connectionName: Short name string matching the connection resource name
field_descriptions.id: ARM ID of the managed API catalog entry — same per region+connector, not per connection
field_descriptions.connectionProperties: Optional — required for MSI-authenticated connections. auth.type: ManagedServiceIdentity

connector_managed_api_ids.note: Case matters. Azuresentinel and Wdatp use capital first letters. All others are lowercase.

msi_connection_arm_pattern.description: Microsoft.Web/connections for MSI-authenticated connectors (Sentinel, WDATP). No credentials in parameterValues.

definition_parameters_pattern.description: Workflow definition parameters for non-secret configurable values.
key_facts: Definition parameters are scoped to the workflow. ARM template parameters are separate.
Only $connections needs a corresponding entry in ARM properties.parameters. Other definition params get values from properties.parameters overrides or fall back to defaultValue.
securestring parameters are redacted from run history. Use them for API keys and passwords.
allowedValues enforces design-time validation only — not runtime enforcement.
metadata.description appears in the designer and ARM template documentation.

implementation_notes: Always create Microsoft.Web/connections resources first and add them to dependsOn in the Logic App resource. Deploying the Logic App before the connection exists causes a runtime authentication failure.
The id field in the $connections value entry must point to the managed API catalog (subscriptionResourceId), NOT to the connection instance. These are different resource IDs.
For MSI-authenticated connections, add connectionProperties: { authentication: { type: ManagedServiceIdentity } } to the connection entry. This field is absent for OAuth connections.
OAuth-based connections (Office 365, SharePoint, Teams) must be manually authorized by a user after deployment. Automate only MSI-based connections end-to-end.
Use ARM template parameters (securestring) to pass credentials to Microsoft.Web/connections.properties.parameterValues. Never hardcode credentials in ARM templates.
For Azuresentinel and Wdatp, the managed API id in the catalog uses capital first letters (Azuresentinel, Wdatp). Other connectors use lowercase. Getting the casing wrong causes a deployment failure.
Workflow definition parameters with defaultValue are self-contained. They do not need ARM-level parameter inputs unless you want to override the default at deploy time.
To export a deployed Logic App to ARM: use the Logic App Designer 'Download' → 'ARM template', or az logic workflow show --out json. The exported template includes the definition and connection IDs.
common_failures: Missing dependsOn on the Logic App resource — connection is not yet created when the Logic App is provisioned, causing a deployment race.
id in $connections value pointing to the connection instance instead of the catalog — runtime auth failure.
connectionId pointing to the catalog instead of the instance — runtime auth failure.
Wrong casing in managed API id (azuresentinel instead of Azuresentinel) — ARM fails to resolve the API.
OAuth connection not authorized after deployment — Logic App runs but connector actions return 401.
Missing $connections in definition.parameters — the @parameters('$connections') expression resolves to null at runtime.
Deploying the Logic App with state: Disabled and forgetting to enable it — Logic App never runs.
Using string instead of securestring for credential parameters — values appear in ARM deployment history and run history.
